stimulus.title: Runbook Retention case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define startup, pause, incident, partial output, retention, deletion, correction, restart, rollback, and escalation procedures with owners. Artifact under review: operable privacy-aware crawler service.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Runbook Retention for operable privacy-aware crawler service.
The record names the assumption, applies Runbook Retention, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Runbook Retention record says operable privacy-aware crawler service looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Runbook Retention.
